filename: 01. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (64).mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\01. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (64).mp3
description: It's a classic but I think it's kinda boring to listen to.
title: Main Theme
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 02. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (Melee).mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\02. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (Melee).mp3
description: It's iconic because of the Melee tournament legal stage but I think it's the weakest of the 3 Melee Pokemon mixes. Just this particular theme is pretty boring.
title: Main Theme
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 03. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (Brawl).mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\03. Main Theme - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue (Brawl).mp3
description: I love the opening of this, the way they used it to announce Charizard in Smash 4, great. But the rest of the track is boring.
title: Main Theme
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 04. Road to Viridian City - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\04. Road to Viridian City - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
description: I really struggle with this remix, because the original track is so goofy. The echo-ey guitar part is fun but really what's going on here.
title: Road to Viridian City
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 05. Pokémon Center - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\05. Pokémon Center - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
description: Everyone knows it but this remix feels very forced.
title: Pokémon Center
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 06. Pokémon Gym & Evolution - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\06. Pokémon Gym & Evolution - Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue.mp3
description: It's a classic track but I feel like the instruments sound really shit. The evolution part doesn't even sound that good.
title: Pokémon Gym/Evolution
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\07. Pokémon Red & Pokémon Blue Medley.mp3
description: Huge banger and full of great content. For the record this is a mix of Trainer Battle (huge banger), the Gym Leader Battle and Wild Pokemon Battle. It used to play on Pokefloats, a long-lost stage. Great times.
title: Pokémon Red / Pokémon Blue Medley
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\08. Pokémon Gold & Pokémon Silver Medley.mp3
description: Probably my favourite of the Melee Pokemon mixes, this one takes us all the way through Johto, covering a lot of ground with Wild Battle, Trainer Battle, Gym Leader Battle and a taste of the Champion Battle too. What a treat!
title: Pokémon Gold / Pokémon Silver Medley
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 09. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Ruby & Pokémon Sapphire.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\09. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Ruby & Pokémon Sapphire.mp3
description: This one's alright! It's kinda forgettable. Cool that there's at least some stuff from Ruby Sapphire.
title: Battle! (Wild Pokémon)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 10. Victory Road - Pokémon Ruby & Pokémon Sapphire.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\10. Victory Road - Pokémon Ruby & Pokémon Sapphire.mp3
description: Huge track! They went so hard on this one. Very memorable hearing this in Subspace Emissary. Great choice of track to remix.
title: Victory Road
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 12. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Diamond & Pokémon Pearl.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\12. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Diamond & Pokémon Pearl.mp3
description: This will always remind me of the Abandoned Zoo level of Subspace where you play as Squirtle and Lucas. I like it but I don't love it.
title: Battle! (Wild Pokémon)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 11. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Diamond & Pokémon Pearl.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\11. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Diamond & Pokémon Pearl.mp3
description: You know they replaced the Brawl remix with this one in Smash 4, and then in Ultimate they put the Brawl one back and this one got cut. What's the real story here? Anyway despite this remix being kinda weird with all of this composer's particular quirks and samples, I actually really enjoy the discordant string chords, the bell sounds, the beat somehow. I just like it.
title: Battle! (Wild Pokémon)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\13. Battle! (Team Galactic).mp3
description: Great music from the amazing Gen 4. The remix is appropriate, has some cool drums, but doesn't take this track to the heights it deserves.
title: Battle! (Team Galactic)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\14. Route 209 - Pokémon Diamond & Pokémon Pearl.mp3
description: This one is such a vibe. When you got slow songs like this back in Brawl it was special. Smashing to this music is great. Also the music is great.
title: Route 209
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\15. Battle! (Dialga & Palkia) & Spear Pillar.mp3
description: This is when I realised that Pokemon music can be EPIC epic. Love the piano in this piece. They really took the original and ran with it. I also love the part with Spear Pillar. It's just great.
title: Battle! (Dialga/Palkia) / Spear Pillar
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\16. Battle! (Champion) & Champion Cynthia.mp3
description: This music put a lot of people onto Cynthia's theme. Honestly, playing so much on Kalos and hearing the fucking solo piano go crazy in tournament was the best. It's so great. Brings back great memories of defeating her for the first time as a child. It was such a huge moment, because it was the first time and only time that Pokemon was hard.
title: Battle! (Champion) / Champion Cynthia
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\17. Route 10 - Pokémon Black & Pokémon White.mp3
description: Yoko Shimomura has a great taste in music, she did a great job with hidden gem from Black/White. I love the acoustic and intense vibe, with the classic Yoko violins too. This could be Professor Layton music.
title: Route 10 - Pokémon Black / Pokémon White
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\18. N's Castle.mp3
description: N's Castle was a great stage on 3DS. What's great about this remix is the music box at the start, but the rest is pretty plain.
title: N's Castle
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\19. Battle! (Reshiram & Zekrom).mp3
description: I feel like this remix isn't that exciting, which is a massive shame, because the original is a huge banger. For me it's a real downgrade, it's missing that huge beat. It does include a nice little nod to Dragonspiral Tower as well.
title: Battle! (Reshiram/Zekrom)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\20. Route 23 - Pokémon Black 2 & Pokémon White 2.mp3
description: Such a specific track they decided to remix from these games. I find it to be very forgettable.
title: Route 23
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 21. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\21. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
description: It's pretty unremarkable.
title: Battle! (Wild Pokémon)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 22. Battle! (Trainer Battle) - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\22. Battle! (Trainer Battle) - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
description: Pretty good remix that makes me think of the Greninja reveal trailer. I've kinda heard it to death on Kalos though.
title: Battle! (Trainer Battle)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\23. Battle! (Team Flare).mp3
description: This remix actually slaps! The melody is so extreme, the bassline is fun. Super intense.
title: Battle! (Team Flare)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\24. Lumiose City.mp3
description: I kinda hate it. Lumiose City is lame.
title: Lumiose City
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

filename: 25. Victory Road - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\25. Victory Road - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
description: It's alright in the sense that it's in 7/4 but it's a short loop and has nothing on the Ruby Sapphire Victory Road.
title: Victory Road
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\26. Battle! (Champion) - Pokémon X & Pokémon Y.mp3
description: This original track is genuinely good. One of the better champion themes! It's different to most of them, it's quite regal. I like the bells. Nice track for Kalos although I've heard it too many times.
title: Battle! (Champion)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\27. Battle! (Steven).mp3
description: I genuinely really enjoy this song! It's a fun little riff and the music sounds good. Great to get this reference to Ruby Sapphire.
title: Battle! (Steven)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon
original-game: Pokémon Omega Ruby & Pokémon Alpha Sapphire

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\28. Battle! (Lorekeeper Zinnia).mp3
description: I remember the hype when someone noticed in some pre-release footage that this song is in the game. It's a banger, but I've heard it just so many times on PS2 that I can no longer truly appreciate it.
title: Battle! (Lorekeeper Zinnia)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon
original-game: Pokémon Omega Ruby & Pokémon Alpha Sapphire

filename: 29. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Sun & Pokémon Moon.mp3
path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\29. Battle! (Wild Pokémon) - Pokémon Sun & Pokémon Moon.mp3
description: Maybe because I've heard it so many times, this one doesn't inspire much in me.
title: Battle! (Wild Pokémon)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\30. Battle! (Trainer) - Pokémon Sun & Pokémon Moon.mp3
description: I've heard this a million times but I genuinely really like it. I remember loving it in Sun and Moon for its special 'B section' where it gets all space-y. This remix does it justice, but in my opinion it could have gone harder. It's got this weird bass note with the distorted guitar during that part which sounds off.
title: Battle! (Trainer)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\31. Battle! (Island Kahuna).mp3
description: This game's version of a gym leader battle that sounds more like a cheesy Jpop song.
title: Battle! (Island Kahuna)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\32. Battle! (Gladion).mp3
description: They controversially took this funky and intense song and made it kinda chill. The original is better but I quite like it.
title: Battle! (Gladion)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\33. Battle! (Elite Four) & Battle! (Solgaleo & Lunala).mp3
description: The Elite Four battle is such an insanely intense track, kudos to them for picking it for remix! The part about a minute it when it sounds like the apocalypse? Kudos. This is the one that people tend to pick in tournament. The Solgaleo/Lunala part works quite well in the context. I've just heard this track too much though, that's the problem.
title: Battle! (Elite Four) / Battle! (Solgaleo/Lunala)
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon

path: music/smash\Super Smash Bros. Anthology Vol. 10 - Pokémon (N64, Wii, 3DS, GC, Wii U, Switch) (gamerip) (1999)\34. The Battle at the Summit!.mp3
description: This one is a bit boring. Imagine having the most intense music ever for the elite four and then just this Pokemon battle in your garden with your neighbour ass music as the champion battle. The remix is also nothing special.
title: The Battle at the Summit!
album: Super Smash Bros. Anthology - Vol. 10: Pokémon
